200	// harnessAliases maps convenience names to canonical harness names.
201	// "local" always routes to the embedded agent; it must never
202	// fall through to a cloud harness like claude or codex.
203	var harnessAliases = map[string]string{
204		"local": "fiz",
205	}
206	
207	// ResolveHarnessAlias returns the canonical harness name for an alias,
208	// or the input unchanged if it is not an alias.
209	func ResolveHarnessAlias(name string) string {
210		if canonical, ok := harnessAliases[name]; ok {
211			return canonical
212		}
213		return name
214	}
215	
216	// Registry manages known harnesses.
217	type Registry struct {
218		LookPath  LookPathFunc
219		harnesses map[string]HarnessConfig
220	}
221	
222	// NewRegistry creates a registry with builtin harnesses.
223	func NewRegistry() *Registry {
224		r := &Registry{
225			LookPath:  DefaultLookPath,
226			harnesses: make(map[string]HarnessConfig),
227		}
228		for k, v := range builtinHarnesses {
229			r.harnesses[k] = v
230		}
231		return r
232	}
233	
234	// Get returns a harness config by name.
235	func (r *Registry) Get(name string) (HarnessConfig, bool) {
236		h, ok := r.harnesses[name]
237		return h, ok
238	}
239	
240	// Has returns true if the harness is registered.
241	func (r *Registry) Has(name string) bool {
242		_, ok := r.harnesses[name]
243		return ok
244	}
245	
246	// Names returns all registered harness names in preference order.
247	func (r *Registry) Names() []string {
248		var names []string
249		// First add preferred harnesses that exist in registry
250		for _, name := range PreferenceOrder {
251			if _, ok := r.harnesses[name]; ok {
252				names = append(names, name)
253			}
254		}
255		// Then add any extras not in preference list
256		for name := range r.harnesses {
257			found := false
258			for _, pref := range PreferenceOrder {
259				if name == pref {
260					found = true
261					break
262				}
263			}
264			if !found {
265				names = append(names, name)
266			}
267		}
268		return names
269	}
270	
271	// Discover checks which harnesses are available on the system.
272	func (r *Registry) Discover() []HarnessStatus {
273		var statuses []HarnessStatus
274		lookPath := r.LookPath
275		if lookPath == nil {
276			lookPath = DefaultLookPath
277		}
278		for _, name := range r.Names() {
279			h := r.harnesses[name]
280			status := HarnessStatus{
281				Name:   name,
282				Binary: h.Binary,
283			}
284			// Embedded harnesses are always available — no binary lookup needed.
285			if name == "virtual" || name == "fiz" || name == "script" {
286				status.Available = true
287				status.Path = "(embedded)"
288			} else if h.IsHTTPProvider {
289				// HTTP-only providers: availability determined by probe, not binary.
290				status.Available = true
291				status.Path = "(http)"
292			} else if path, err := lookPath(h.Binary); err != nil {
293				status.Available = false
294				status.Error = "binary not found"
295			} else {
296				status.Available = true
297				status.Path = path
298			}
299			statuses = append(statuses, status)
300		}
301		return statuses
302	}
303	
304	// FirstAvailable returns the first available harness in preference order.
305	func (r *Registry) FirstAvailable() (string, bool) {
306		for _, s := range r.Discover() {
307			if s.Available {
308				return s.Name, true
309			}
310		}
311		return "", false
312	}

82	// HarnessEntry is the harness-side input the caller (service) supplies.
83	// It is the routing engine's view of a registered harness; the engine does
84	// not import the harnesses package directly to keep the dependency narrow.
85	type HarnessEntry struct {
86		Name                string
87		Surface             string
88		CostClass           string
89		IsLocal             bool
90		IsSubscription      bool
91		IsHTTPProvider      bool
92		AutoRoutingEligible bool
93		TestOnly            bool
94		ExactPinSupport     bool
95		DefaultModel        string
96		SupportedModels     []string
97		// AutoRoutingModels enumerates the concrete model IDs the engine may rank
98		// when a caller pins Harness but leaves Model empty. This lets harness-local
99		// policy routing choose within the harness instead of being forced onto a
100		// single DefaultModel. Nil falls back to DefaultModel resolution.
101		AutoRoutingModels  []string
102		SupportedReasoning []string
103		MaxReasoningTokens int
104		SupportedPerms     []string
105		SupportsTools      bool
106	
107		// Available reflects the harness's discovered availability.
108		Available bool
109	
110		// QuotaOK / QuotaPercentUsed reflect live quota state (when applicable).
111		// SubscriptionOK gates subscription harnesses at the eligibility level:
112		// when false, the candidate is ineligible regardless of score.
113		QuotaOK          bool
114		QuotaPercentUsed int
115		QuotaStale       bool
116		QuotaTrend       string // unknown|healthy|burning|exhausting
117		QuotaReason      string
118		SubscriptionOK   bool // false = hard gate; true = score-based demotion
119	
120		// InCooldown marks the entire harness as being in a failure cooldown.
121		// When true the harness is demoted in score (via candidateInternal.InCooldown)
122		// but not hard-rejected, so it can still win when all other harnesses are
123		// also unavailable.
124		InCooldown bool
125	
126		// Providers is the list of providers this harness can dispatch to.
127		// For subprocess harnesses (claude/codex) this is typically a single
128		// vendor entry. For the native "fiz" harness it is the configured
129		// list of HTTP providers.
130		Providers []ProviderEntry
131	}
132	
133	// ProviderEntry describes one provider available under a harness.
134	type ProviderEntry struct {
135		Name                 string
136		BaseURL              string
137		ServerInstance       string
138		EndpointName         string
139		EndpointBaseURL      string
140		DefaultModel         string
141		Billing              modelcatalog.BillingModel
142		CostClass            string
143		DiscoveredIDs        []string // models discovered via /v1/models or equivalent
144		DiscoveryAttempted   bool
145		ContextWindows       map[string]int
146		ContextWindowSources map[string]string
147		ContextWindow        int
148		ContextWindowSource  string
149		SupportsTools        bool
150		SupportsToolsByModel map[string]bool
151	
152		// CostUSDPer1kTokens is the estimated blended USD cost per 1,000 tokens.
153		// A zero value with CostSourceUnknown means the provider cost is unknown.
154		CostUSDPer1kTokens float64
155		// CostSource describes where CostUSDPer1kTokens came from: catalog,
156		// subscription, user-config, or unknown.
157		CostSource string
158		// ActualCashSpend marks providers that would consume metered spend when
159		// selected.
160		ActualCashSpend bool
161	
162		// InCooldown reflects whether this provider is in a failure-cooldown window.
163		InCooldown bool
164	
165		// ExcludeFromDefaultRouting, when true, prevents this provider from being
166		// selected during unpinned automatic routing. An explicit provider, harness,
167		// or model pin bypasses this gate so the operator can still reach the
168		// provider intentionally. Corresponds to IncludeByDefault=false in config.
169		ExcludeFromDefaultRouting bool
170	}
171	
172	const (
173		// CostSourceCatalog means cost came from the model catalog.
174		CostSourceCatalog = "catalog"
175		// CostSourceSubscription means cost came from subscription quota pricing.
176		CostSourceSubscription = "subscription"
177		// CostSourceUnknown means no reliable cost estimate is available.
178		CostSourceUnknown = "unknown"
179		// CostSourceUserConfig means cost came from explicit user configuration.
180		CostSourceUserConfig = "user-config"
181	)
182	
183	// Decision is the routing engine's output: the picked candidate plus the
184	// full ranked list (including rejected ones with rejection reasons).
185	type Decision struct {
186		Harness        string
187		Provider       string
188		Endpoint       string
189		ServerInstance string
190		Model          string
191		Reason         string
192		Candidates     []Candidate
193	}
194	
195	// Candidate is one ranked routing option.
196	type Candidate struct {
197		Harness            string
198		Provider           string
199		Billing            modelcatalog.BillingModel
200		Endpoint           string
201		ServerInstance     string
202		Model              string
203		Score              float64
204		CostUSDPer1kTokens float64
205		CostSource         string
206		ActualCashSpend    bool
207		Power              int
208		ContextLength      int
209		ContextSource      string
210		ScoreComponents    map[string]float64
211		Eligible           bool
212		Reason             string
213	
214		// FilterReason is the typed disqualification category, set at the
215		// rejection site that decided why this candidate is ineligible.
216		// Empty for eligible candidates. Service-layer code maps this to the
217		// public FilterReason* string constants without parsing free-form
218		// Reason text.
219		FilterReason FilterReason
220	
221		// LatencyMS, SuccessRate, and CostClass expose the score-component
222		// inputs so callers can render per-axis explanations alongside the
223		// final Score. Zero / negative values mean unknown (see Inputs docs).
224		LatencyMS       float64
225		SuccessRate     float64
226		CostClass       string
227		SpeedTPS        float64
228		Utilization     float64
229		ContextHeadroom int
230	
231		QuotaOK          bool
232		QuotaPercentUsed int
233		QuotaTrend       string
234		StickyAffinity   float64
235	}
236	
237	// FilterReason categorizes why a routing candidate was disqualified.
238	// The zero value (empty string) means the candidate is eligible.
239	type FilterReason string
240	
241	const (
242		// FilterReasonEligible is the zero value for an eligible candidate.
243		FilterReasonEligible FilterReason = ""
244		// FilterReasonContextTooSmall: candidate's context window is below the
245		// request's MinContextWindow().
246		FilterReasonContextTooSmall FilterReason = "context_too_small"
247		// FilterReasonNoToolSupport: request needs tool calling but candidate
248		// does not support it.
249		FilterReasonNoToolSupport FilterReason = "no_tool_support"
250		// FilterReasonReasoningUnsupported: candidate cannot satisfy the
251		// requested reasoning policy.
252		FilterReasonReasoningUnsupported FilterReason = "reasoning_unsupported"
253		// FilterReasonUnhealthy: harness/provider is unavailable, in cooldown,
254		// out of quota, or excluded by a hard provider-preference gate.
255		FilterReasonUnhealthy FilterReason = "unhealthy"
256		// FilterReasonScoredBelowTop: catch-all for ineligibility that does
257		// not fit a more specific category (also used for capability
258		// mismatches such as permissions/model-pin/exact-pin and for model
259		// resolution failures).
260		FilterReasonScoredBelowTop FilterReason = "scored_below_top"
261		// FilterReasonPinMismatch: candidate was rejected because it does not
262		// satisfy an explicit caller pin such as Provider.
263		FilterReasonPinMismatch FilterReason = "pin_mismatch"
264		// FilterReasonPowerMissing: automatic routing requires catalog power
265		// metadata, but the candidate model is unknown or has zero power.
266		FilterReasonPowerMissing FilterReason = "power_missing"
267		// FilterReasonBelowMinPower: candidate model power is below req.MinPower.
268		FilterReasonBelowMinPower FilterReason = "below_min_power"
269		// FilterReasonAboveMaxPower: candidate model power is above req.MaxPower.
270		FilterReasonAboveMaxPower FilterReason = "above_max_power"
271		// FilterReasonExactPinOnly: catalog marks the model as only eligible for
272		// explicit model pins.
273		FilterReasonExactPinOnly FilterReason = "exact_pin_only"
274		// FilterReasonNotAutoRoutable: catalog metadata exists but marks the
275		// model inactive, deprecated, stale, or otherwise excluded from
276		// automatic routing.
277		FilterReasonNotAutoRoutable FilterReason = "not_auto_routable"
278		// FilterReasonQuotaExhausted: provider is in the quota_exhausted state
279		// with retry_after in the future. The candidate would have been eligible
280		// otherwise.
281		FilterReasonQuotaExhausted FilterReason = "quota_exhausted"

data.text: This bead has 7 ACs spanning multiple subsystems (engine enumeration, per-tier cost metadata, scoring assertion, catalog→harness preference for pinned models, negative filter-reason test). The "ac-quality:needs-refinement" label confirms this is decomposable. Let me split.

data.output:
ID:       fizeau-80d4bf41
Title:    auto-route: enumerate per-tier candidates for multi-tier subscription harnesses (claude opus/sonnet/haiku)
Type:     bug
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   kind:bug, area:routing, area:harness, impact:cost, evidence:downstream-ddx, refs:github-issue-6, ac-quality:needs-refinement
Owner:    erik
Desc:     PROBLEM
Under policy=default (auto-route mode, no --harness/--provider/--model pin), Fizeau collapses each subprocess harness to ONE scoring candidate — its DefaultModel. Multi-tier subscription harnesses like claude (which advertises sonnet/opus/haiku as siblings under one auth) therefore only ever offer their default tier to the auto-route scorer. Sonnet and haiku can never win on cost vs opus even though the catalog has full power/cost metadata for all three.

Concrete symptom from downstream DDx caller: every policy=default dispatch lands on claude/opus-4.7 because internal/harnesses/registry.go:51 sets DefaultModel="opus-4.7". For a 2207-token implementation prompt at role=implementer, opus won score 100.5. When the same dispatch is rerun with --model sonnet-4.6 pinned, sonnet scores 196.0 — it would have beaten opus by 95.5 points if it had been enumerated.

Cost gap is material: opus is 5x sonnet on input tokens, ~5x on output (cost_input_per_m: 15.0 vs 3.0; cost_output_per_m: 75.0 vs 15.0 per internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml). A project running ddx work continuously is defaulting every dispatch to the most expensive tier.

ROOT CAUSE (tentative — pointers, not patch)
service_routing.go:1172-1189 (auto-route candidate add loop):

    for _, h := range entries {
        if h.DefaultModel != "" {
            add(h.DefaultModel, true, status)      // adds opus-4.7 for claude
        }
        for _, modelID := range h.SupportedModels {
            add(modelID, true, status)              // SHOULD add sonnet-4.6 too
        }
        ...
    }

subprocessHarnessModelIDs("claude", cfg) at service_models.go:91-101 returns the full ["sonnet", "sonnet-4.6", "opus", "opus-4.7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"] set. So sonnet SHOULD be reaching the candidate pool. Evidence says it does not — three candidate causes I could not disambiguate without deeper reading of routing.Inputs construction:
1. Downstream aggregation/dedup is keeping one model per {harness, provider} key (picking DefaultModel as representative).
2. Eligibility map keyed by modelID at ~line 1170 is collapsing sonnet entries by family before the candidate list is emitted.

input.command:
ddx bead create "engine: enumerate per-tier AutoRoutingModels for multi-tier subscription harnesses in default policy" \
  --type bug \
  --priority 1 \
  --labels "kind:bug, area:routing, area:harness, impact:cost, evidence:downstream-ddx, refs:github-issue-6, parent:fizeau-80d4bf41" \
  --description $'PROBLEM
Under policy=default (no --harness/--provider/--model pin), the routing engine only emits ONE candidate per subprocess harness (its DefaultModel). For multi-tier subscription harnesses like claude (opus/sonnet/haiku under one auth), sonnet and haiku are never enumerated, so cost-aware scoring cannot pick the cheaper tier.

ROOT CAUSE (verified by reading internal/routing/engine.go:1410)
routingModelsForProvider enumerates h.AutoRoutingModels only when req.Harness != "" (i.e., harness is explicitly pinned). Under default policy with no pins, the function falls through to resolveModel, which returns a single model (DefaultModel).

ALSO needed: applySubscriptionRoutingCost in service_routing.go currently builds a single ProviderEntry with the cost of the DefaultModel only. For per-tier scoring to work, the ProviderEntry must carry per-model cost via ContextWindows-style maps (or equivalent), so the engine's candidateCost path can pick up the correct cost for each model in AutoRoutingModels.

SCOPE
- internal/routing/engine.go routingModelsForProvider: enumerate AutoRoutingModels when no Model/Provider pin AND the harness is a multi-tier subscription harness (h.IsSubscription && len(h.AutoRoutingModels)>1).
- internal/routing or service_routing.go: ensure each enumerated model uses its own catalog cost, not the harness DefaultModel cost.
- New unit tests in internal/routing covering per-tier enumeration for a subscription harness.

NON-SCOPE (deferred to sibling beads under fizeau-80d4bf41)
- Pin --model X routing through subscription harness instead of openrouter fallback.
- Negative test asserting filter_reason for an excluded same-harness tier.

CROSS-REF
Parent: fizeau-80d4bf41' \
  --acceptance $'1. Under policy=default with no --harness/--model pin and a multi-tier claude HarnessEntry (AutoRoutingModels including sonnet+opus), routing.Resolve emits >1 candidate for that harness in Decision.Candidates. Verified by unit test in internal/routing/engine_test.go.
2. Each per-tier candidate carries the correct cost from its catalog entry (sonnet input cost ~3.0 per Mtok, opus ~15.0 per Mtok), not the DefaultModel cost. Verified by asserting Candidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens differs across tiers in the test.
3. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./internal/routing/... ./... green.
4. No regression in service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing/engine_test.go.'

data.output: fizeau-f7fa1d87

input.command:
ddx bead create "routing: pin --model sonnet-4.6 routes through claude subscription harness, not openrouter fallback" \
  --type bug \
  --priority 1 \
  --labels "kind:bug, area:routing, area:harness, impact:cost, refs:github-issue-6, parent:fizeau-80d4bf41" \
  --description $'PROBLEM
When a caller pins --model sonnet-4.6 (no --harness pin) and a claude subscription harness is available, dispatch currently routes to fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 instead of the same-auth claude subscription harness. This means the operator-friendly workaround "pin sonnet by name" does not deliver sonnet on the subscription path; it falls through to a separate per-token provider (which then 401s if openrouter is not authed).

ROOT CAUSE (tentative)
When --model is pinned, the routing engine resolves candidates per (harness, provider, resolved-model). The claude harness exposes sonnet via its SupportedModels list, but harness selection for a pinned model appears to prefer the harness whose surface_id matches the catalog's canonical-by-surface lookup. Need to verify whether the engine prefers subscription harnesses for pinned models that match their SupportedModels.

SCOPE
- Identify where --model X is resolved to (harness, provider) pair for non-pinned-harness requests.
- For subscription harnesses with the pinned model in their SupportedModels/AutoRoutingModels, ensure they appear as a viable candidate (and preferably beat openrouter on score for cost-aware scoring with a healthy subscription quota).
- Unit test with a claude harness mock and openrouter HarnessEntry: pin --model sonnet-4.6, assert decision.Harness == "claude".

NON-SCOPE
- The openrouter 401 itself (separate config issue).
- Multi-tier enumeration under policy=default (sibling bead fizeau-f7fa1d87).

CROSS-REF
Parent: fizeau-80d4bf41' \
  --acceptance $'1. Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against routing.Inputs that include both a healthy claude subscription harness (with sonnet-4.6 in SupportedModels) and an openrouter HarnessEntry (with anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 in DiscoveredIDs) yields Decision.Harness == "claude". Verified by unit test in internal/routing/engine_test.go.
2. The decision's candidate list shows both harnesses as eligible (no silent exclusion of claude on a pinned-by-name request).
3. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./internal/routing/... ./... green.
4. No regression in existing routing tests.'

data.output: fizeau-59df1d15

input.command:
ddx bead create "routing: emit filter_reason for excluded same-harness tier (no silent absence)" \
  --type bug \
  --priority 2 \
  --labels "kind:bug, area:routing, observability, refs:github-issue-6, parent:fizeau-80d4bf41" \
  --description $'PROBLEM
When a candidate from a multi-tier subscription harness is excluded (e.g., the catalog has no power metadata for that tier), the routing_decision event drops the row silently rather than emitting it with an explicit filter_reason. This makes downstream diagnosis impossible — "absent" is ambiguous between "never enumerated" and "filtered out".

SCOPE
- Verify the routing engine emits an ineligible Candidate row (with FilterReason set) when a same-harness tier is known to the harness (in AutoRoutingModels / SupportedModels) but missing required catalog metadata.
- If absent today, ensure buildHarnessCandidates emits a row with eligible=false, filter_reason=FilterReasonPowerMissing (or similar) instead of dropping it.
- Negative test: stub a claude HarnessEntry with sonnet-4.6 enumerated but ModelEligibility lookup returning power=0 for sonnet — assert the candidate appears in Decision.Candidates with FilterReason=FilterReasonPowerMissing.

DEPENDS ON
fizeau-f7fa1d87 (per-tier enumeration must work first).

CROSS-REF
Parent: fizeau-80d4bf41' \
  --acceptance $'1. With a claude HarnessEntry whose AutoRoutingModels include sonnet-4.6 but whose ModelEligibility lookup returns power=0 for sonnet-4.6, routing.Resolve emits a Candidate row for sonnet-4.6 with Eligible=false and FilterReason=FilterReasonPowerMissing (not silent absence).
2. Test in internal/routing/engine_test.go asserts the row is present with the expected FilterReason.
3. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./internal/routing/... ./... green.
4. No regression.'

reason: parent bead has 7 ACs spanning three orthogonal routing subsystems (engine candidate enumeration with per-tier cost metadata; catalog→harness preference for pinned models that match multiple harnesses' SupportedModels; observability invariant that no enumerated tier may silently vanish from routing_decision). Decomposed into three children that each scope to one subsystem and are independently verifiable.

children:
  - fizeau-f7fa1d87: engine: enumerate per-tier AutoRoutingModels for multi-tier subscription harnesses in default policy (parent ACs 1, 2, 3)
  - fizeau-59df1d15: routing: pin --model sonnet-4.6 routes through claude subscription harness, not openrouter fallback (parent AC 4)

rationale for split (Step 0 criteria):
  - 7 ACs mixing implementation (1, 2), scoring/integration assertion (3), separate-codepath dispatch destination (4), observability invariant (5), and project-wide test gates (6, 7).

  - Estimated combined surface: ~3 packages (internal/routing, root fizeau, internal/serviceimpl) with new unit and integration tests; better delivered in three reviewable commits than one.

Decomposition complete. Per the bead instructions, decomposition alone is success — I do not mix it with implementation, and I do not commit. Stopping here.

**Summary:**

  - `fizeau-f7fa1d87` — engine: enumerate per-tier AutoRoutingModels in default policy (parent ACs 1–3)

- Dep edges recorded; parent notes updated; `no_changes_rationale.txt` written to bundle path with `status: open` + `orchestrator_action: decompose`.
